feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Pass unnamed inbound parameter to a CAP CDS action
entry.content:
Dear community,

I am trying to build a CAP CDS service where it would be helpful if I could benefit from a custom-defined type as an inbound action parameter type in BUT without the need to specify the parameter name. For example:  

type Type1 
 {acknowledge: String;
      message: String; 
  }

  action Action1 (Type1 ) returns String;

Do you think it is possible?

Best regards

entry.title: Reload an Object Page with a different record after a custom action
entry.content:
Greetings, community experts.

I am working on a SAP CAP app (NodeJS) with SAP Fiori Elements on SAP BTP. At the SAP Fiori Elements level I am also using some Extension.

What I want to do is simple to explain: After executing certain custom action on an Object Page, I need to reload that page, but with changing the key, to display another object, i.e. another record of the same entity... For example, the Object Page is displaying Batch 10001, and after the custom action, the object page should reload and display Batch 10015.

How could I do this?

Thanks in advance for any help and/or suggestions.

entry.title: @sap/cap-rfc update to use a RFC websocket destination
entry.content: Will there be an update to the @sap/cap-rfc library to use a destination from the btp destination service, that is configured as RFC Websocket, since the Cloud Connector 2.19 allows such connections? This would make it easier for applications to use secured rfc connections with principal propagation without configuring SNC.

entry.title: Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigates to transient $uid path instead of server draft path,
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am facing an issue in a CAP + Fiori Elements OData V4 draft application

Problem:

 When I press Create in one specific config app (AggrConfigService / Aggregator), the draft creation flow breaks.

The UI navigates/binds to a transient client-side path like: AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)

CAP then returns: 404 Invalid resource path "AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=...)" What is strange is that the POST itself succeeds first.

Observed request/response

1) Create request: POST Aggregator Body: {"AggregatorID":"Test"}

Response: HTTP/1.1 204 No Content location: Aggregator.drafts('Test') 2) Immediately after that, UI5 sends: GET Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)?$select=AggregatorID,AggregatorName,HasActiveEntity,HasDraftEntity,IsActive,IsActiveEntity&$expand=DraftAdministrativeData($select=DraftIsCreatedByMe,DraftUUID,InProcessByUser) Response: HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found {"error":{"code":"404","message":"Invalid resource path \"AggrConfigService.Aggregator($uid=id-1773199858692-511)\""}}

So the backend seems to create the draft successfully and returns a draft location, but the frontend still tries to read via the transient $uid path instead of switching to the canonical draft path.

Environment:

SAP CAP Node.js - @sap/cds 8.9.8 - SAPUI5 1.142.0 - Fiori Elements OData V4 - Draft-enabled service - Cloud Foundry deployment on BTP

Questions:

1) Has anyone seen Fiori Elements OData V4 draft create navigate to a transient `$uid` path like this after a successful POST?

2) Is there any known issue/pattern where FE object-page routing or binding keeps the transient context instead of resolving to the canonical draft location returned by CAP?

Thanks a lot.

Best Regards,

Muthu

SAPUI5 SAP Fiori Elements SAP Cloud Application Programming Model  OData SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: PostgreSQL Schema Evolution in SAP CAP - How to Handle DROP and RENAME Operations
entry.content:
I've been working with SAP CAP and @cap-js/postgres , and discovered a critical gap in schema evolution capabilities. CAP's schema_evolution: 'alter' mode intentionally avoids destructive operations (DROP, RENAME) for safety reasons, but this creates real operational challenges in production deployments.

The problem: When you need to remove a column or rename a table, CAP won't handle it automatically. This forces either:
- Manual SQL scripts (error-prone)
- Database downtime for deployments
- Schema drift issues

I've created a solution called @cds-pgroll/postgres  that bridges this gap. It runs as a pre-deployment step using pgroll (inspired by Shopify's approach) to safely handle destructive operations before CAP's standard schema sync.

Key features:
- Automatic CSN delta detection (compares deployed vs. new schema)
- Supports: DROP table/column, RENAME table/column, add constraints
- 2-phase deployment pipeline with Cloud Foundry MTA integration
- Zero-downtime migrations for most operations

If anyone has faced similar challenges with CAP schema evolution, or has feedback on this approach, I'd love to hear your experiences and use cases.

GitHub: https://github.com/Medelkouch98/cds-pgroll
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@cds-pgroll/postgres

entry.title: 401 Unauthorized when sending notifications from CAP using SAP_Notifications destination
entry.content:
I am creating a CAP application to send notifications and configured the SAP_Notifications destination according to the documentation below.

When I call the API using Postman, it works correctly, but when I try to call it from my application, it always returns 401 Unauthorized.

I also tried calling it directly using the SAP Cloud SDK, following this documentation: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/developing-cloud-foundry-applications-with-notifications

Documentation used to configure the destination:
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/enabling-notifications-for-custom-apps-on-sap-btp-cloud-foundry#configure-the-destination-to-the-notifications-service

Additionally, I tried using @cap-js/notifications, but I always receive 401, both locally and after deployment.

Does anyone know what could be causing this or how to fix it?

Additional information:

  • Destination configuration screenshot

  • Postman request working screenshot

The configurations are the same as the ones generated in Work Zone for the notification service.

The BTP logs show a 401 Unauthorized error during the content deployment process in BTP.

If anyone has faced this issue before, I would appreciate any guidance.

entry.title: Running Hugging Face AI Models with SAPUI5 and CAPM
entry.content:
Hi everyone 👋

After spending several hours researching and experimenting, I finally found a simple way to answer a question that many SAP developers have started asking:

“Is it possible to run AI models inside SAP applications using SAPUI5 and CAPM?”

The short answer is:

   Yes, absolutely!

In this blog, I will share how we can integrate Hugging Face AI models with SAPUI5 and CAPM, and how we can build a simple AI-powered application that can generate text responses and even images.

 

                                                                        Fig 1.1 Hugging Face Models sections

Now the Interesting Part: Can SAPUI5 Use AI?

        When I started this experiment, my first question was:

Can SAPUI5 directly call an AI model?

        The answer is yes, especially for text generation models.

To make this work, we only need three things:

- A Hugging Face account
-  An HF API Token
-  A model endpoint

Once you log in to Hugging Face, you can generate an HF Token from your account settings.

Then you can choose any model depending on your use case. Hugging Face provides model benchmarks and descriptions, so you can easily decide which one is best for:

  • Education
  • Coding
  • Reasoning
  • Chat
  • Content generation

Connecting Hugging Face with SAPUI5

Now let's talk about the implementation.

In the SAPUI5 controller, we can send a request to the Hugging Face API using JavaScript fetch().

The UI simply takes a user prompt, sends it to the AI model, and displays the response.

Example flow:

User enters a prompt:

       What is Hugging Face and SAPUI5?

SAPUI5 sends the prompt to the Hugging Face model.

The model generates a response and returns it.

Then SAPUI5 displays the result directly on the UI.

This makes it very easy to build AI chat assistants inside SAP applications.

 

                                                                                    Fig 1.2 Output- Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

Sample Code:

              Fig 1.3 sapui5 Controller.js - Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

                      Fig 1.3 sapui5 view.xml - Hugging Face Ai Chat

But What About Image Generation?

      This is where things become interesting.

      When I tried running image generation models directly in SAPUI5, I realized something important.

      SAPUI5 works best with text-based AI APIs.

      For image generation models, we need a backend service.

This is where CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model) becomes very useful.

 

Using CAPM for Image Generation

To generate images, we can integrate fal.ai, which provides inference APIs for advanced image generation models.

Some examples include:

  • black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev
  • Tongyi-MAI / Z-Image-Turbo

These models can generate high-quality AI images from text prompts.

For example:

"Portrait of a futuristic astronaut with glowing blue eyes"

The system sends this prompt to the AI model, and the model generates an image.

 

How CAPM Handles This

Inside the CAPM Node.js service, we create a custom action such as:

     generateImage

This action receives the prompt from SAPUI5.

Then CAPM calls the fal.ai API with that prompt.

The AI model generates the image and returns a URL of the generated image, which we send back to the UI.

This makes CAPM act as a bridge between SAPUI5 and AI models.

 

Why Use fal.ai Instead of Hugging Face Directly?

You might ask:

If we already use Hugging Face, why do we need fal.ai?

   Good question.

Some models listed on Hugging Face are actually hosted through different inference providers, including fal.ai.

For example:

"black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev"

This model is often executed through fal.ai inference APIs.

So fal.ai becomes an easy way to run those models with strong GPU performance.

Sample Code:

                   Fig 1.4 Output – CAPM - Hugging face Image generation

 

 

                  Fig 1.5 service.js - first half- Hugging face Image generation

 

                  Fig 1.6 service.js - second half - Hugging face Image generation

 

               Fig 1.7 Fal.ai Api key Generation - Hugging face Image generation

 

Final Result

After connecting everything together, we get a simple AI application:

The application can:

✅Generate AI text responses using SAPUI5
✅Generate AI images using CAPM services
✅Use Hugging Face models
✅Run without training your own model

All of this can be done using free developer APIs, unless you need large-scale GPU workloads.

 

Conclusion

From this experiment, we can see that SAP applications can easily integrate with modern AI platforms.

The architecture becomes very simple:

                                         SAPUI5
                                              ⬇
                                  CAPM Service
                                             ⬇
                    Hugging Face / fal.ai AI Models

 

Using this approach, developers can build powerful applications such as:

  • AI chat assistants inside SAP
  • AI-powered knowledge systems
  • Image generation tools
  • Smart enterprise automation

And the best part is that you can even deploy your own AI model on Hugging Face and use it inside SAP applications.

 

⭐I hope this small experiment helps other developers who are curious about AI + SAP integration.

Happy coding! 🚀

entry.title: CAP CDS: customize error response structure before OPEN API generation
entry.content:
Hi community,

I trying to generate an OpenAPI documentation from a CAP CDS service. I was wondering if there is any way to influence the Error Response structure before generation (an annotation maybe).

Any hints from your side will be appreciated.

Many thanks and kind regards,

Tesnim

entry.title: How to: Use OpenText Core Archive for Document Storage/Archiving from SAP BTP / Build?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are designing a solution on SAP BTP using SAP Build / CAP and SAP Build Process Automation and would like to use OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions as our document storage/archiving platform – without involving an SAP ECC/S/4HANA backend for the document upload.

Scenario:

  • We have a SAP Fiori / CAP front-end application with a file upload (PDF) functionality deployed in Cloud Foundry / BTP.
  • Uploaded PDFs should:
    1. Be stored/archived centrally in OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions.
    2. Be retrievable later in SAP Build Process Automation to show the document (PDF) in an approval form for review/approval.
  • We want to call OpenText directly from BTP, not via ECC/S/4HANA, and then access those documents via APIs from workflows in SAP Build Process Automation.

Questions:

  • OpenText Core Archive APIs from BTP

    • Does OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions expose REST APIs that can be called directly from BTP (CAP/Node/Java) for:
      • Creating (uploading) documents (PDF) with metadata?
      • Retrieving documents (either as binary or via a time-limited URL)?
  • Or is there a more “standard” pattern for using Core Archive as a central document store from BTP without ECC/S/4?
  • Are there any official guidelines or recommendations from SAP/OpenText for this type of BTP ↔ Core Archive integration?
  • Even if examples are for Extended ECM or other OpenText products, any patterns applicable to Core Archive would be helpful.

Any inputs, experiences, example architectures, or links to documentation/blogs are highly appreciated.

Thanks! in Advance.

BR,
Rakesh

entry.title: Application Frontend Service app with IAS leads to 401
entry.content:
We're building an integration monitoring application on SAP BTP using SAP CAP (Node.js) as the backend and a SAPUI5 Fiori Elements app as the frontend. For deployment, we chose the Application Frontend Service (app-front) as a managed approuter — instead of deploying and maintaining our own approuter module.

Is there any example of Application Frontend Service with IAS or also with your own CAP backend? I don't know what is broken.

For authentication, we deliberately went with IAS (Identity Authentication Service) only — no XSUAA.

This mta.yaml let's you deploy, but will end up with 401 Unauthorized calls to the backend in the browser logs of the deployed frontend app:

 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: cpi-monitoring
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: cpi-monitoring-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    properties:
      AMS_DCL_ROOT: ams/dcl
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
      - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
        parameters:
          config:
            app-identifier: srv
            credential-type: X509_GENERATED
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-cert-url: ${protocol}://${default-host}.cert.${default-domain}
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      instances: 1
      routes:
        - route: ${default-url}
        - route: ${default-host}.cert.${default-domain}
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm-ci
    deployed-after:
      - cpi-monitoring-ams-policies-deployer
  - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres-deployer
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/pg
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      no-route: true
      no-start: true
      tasks:
        - command: npm start
          name: deploy-to-postgresql
  - name: cpi-monitoring-ams-policies-deployer
    type: javascript.nodejs
    path: gen/policies
    requires:
      - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
        parameters:
          config:
            app-identifier: ams-policy-deployer
            credential-type: X509_GENERATED
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      no-route: true
      no-start: true
      tasks:
        - command: npm start
          memory: 512M
          name: deploy-dcl
  # - name: cpi-monitoring-app-content # for Work Zone deployment
  #   type: com.sap.application.content
  #   path: .
  #   requires:
  #     - name: cpi-monitoring-repo-host
  #       parameters:
  #         content-target: true
  #   build-parameters:
  #     build-result: resources
  #     requires:
  #       - artifacts:
  #           - monitoring.zip
  #         name: monitoring
  #         target-path: resources/
  # - name: monitoring
  #   type: html5
  #   path: app/monitoring
  #   build-parameters:
  #     build-result: dist
  #     builder: custom
  #     commands:
  #       - npm install
  #       - npm run build:cf
  #       - npm ci
  #       - npm run build
  #     supported-platforms:
  #       []

  - name: cpi-monitoring-app-deployer
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - monitoring.zip
          name: cpimonitoringmonitoring
          target-path: resources/
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
      - name: cpi-monitoring-app-front
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    parameters:
      config:
        destinations:
          - name: monitoring
            url: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
            forwardAuthToken: true
            forwardAuthCertificates: true
          - name: ui5
            url: https://ui5.sap.com

  - name: cpimonitoringmonitoring
    type: html5
    path: app/monitoring
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: cpi-monitoring-postgres
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: postgresql-db
      service-plan: free
  - name: cpi-monitoring-ias
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        authorization:
          enabled: true
        display-name: cpi-monitoring
        oauth2-configuration:
          token-policy:
            access-token-format: jwt
        provided-apis:
          - description: API exposed by the application
            name: cpi-monitoring-ias-api
        xsuaa-cross-consumption: true
      service: identity
      service-name: cpi-monitoring-ias
      service-plan: application
  # - name: cpi-monitoring-repo-host # for Work Zone deployment
  #   type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  #   parameters:
  #     service: html5-apps-repo
  #     service-name: cpi-monitoring-html5-service
  #     service-plan: app-host
  - name: cpi-monitoring-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: cpi-monitoring-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
  - name: cpi-monitoring-app-front
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: app-front
      service-plan: developer
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

 

 

Destination name mismatch (404 on OData calls)

xs-app.json routes OData requests to destination: "monitoring". We initially had the destination named srv-api in the app-deployer config. Result: every OData call returned 404 with a message like:

Error while retrieving destination monitoring from destination service. HTTP status code: 404

Fix was: rename the destination to monitoring — exactly matching xs-app.json.

401 Unauthorized from CAP backend

After fixing the 404, we got 401 from the backend on every OData request. The app-front debug logs showed:

Exchange token required = false

When I try to change things the deployment often fails like this:

[cpi-monitoring-destination-content] [ERROR] Unexpected error occurred

entry.title: What should enterprise teams learn from the recent Axios supply chain incident?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I wanted to start a broader discussion about dependency trust and supply chain risk after the recent Axios incident, where compromised npm releases were reported for axios 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. Public reporting says those versions were maliciously published, and the Axios GitHub issue tracked the incident as it unfolded. StepSecurity also reported that the malicious releases were removed from npm and that the latest dist-tag reverted to 1.14.0.

What makes this especially concerning is that Axios is not some obscure package. It is a very widely used dependency, directly or indirectly, across many enterprise stacks. That means even if your own project does not explicitly add Axios, you may still inherit that risk through frameworks, SDKs, or platform libraries used in corporate environments. The npm page for @sap/cds shows how central SAP’s CAP tooling is in many Node.js-based enterprise projects, which makes this kind of dependency-chain discussion very relevant for SAP developers too.

For me, this raises a few important questions:

  • Are we still too quick to auto-update dependencies just because they come from popular packages?
  • Should enterprise teams introduce a waiting period before adopting freshly published versions of critical libraries?
  • How much visibility do we really have into transitive dependencies used by frameworks and vendor packages?
  • Should vendors and platform teams communicate more clearly when widely used dependencies are involved in incidents like this?

In SAP-related projects, this topic feels even more important because many teams rely heavily on trusted platform libraries and may assume that popular dependencies are inherently safe. But incidents like this show that popularity is not a security guarantee. A single compromised release in a common package can affect a very large ecosystem in a very short time.

I would be interested in hearing how others handle this in practice:

  • Do you pin versions more aggressively now?
  • Do you block newly released packages for some time before adoption?
  • Do you audit transitive dependencies regularly?
  • Should enterprise vendors like SAP be more conservative about dependency updates after incidents like this?

I think this is a good moment for all of us to revisit how much trust we place in open-source dependencies, especially in enterprise software landscapes.

 

Fonts: 

https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/10604 (Official)

https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/axios-compromised-on-npm-malicious-versions-drop-remote-access-trojan

entry.title: Dynamic Tile KPI in SAP Build Work Zone routes to HTML5 runtime destination instead of CAP backend
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am facing an issue while configuring a dynamic KPI tile in SAP Build Work Zone. In my project, the site is deployed using CDM (cdm.json) to define the site structure, apps, and tiles. The KPI is configured in the application manifest.json through indicatorDataSource, as described in the SAP documentation and this blog :

 

"indicatorDataSource": {
	"dataSource": "<service>",
	"path": "<handler>",
	"refresh": 600
}

 

The tile shows "... Error" and the request fails with a 500 Internal Server Error. From the browser Network tab, I can see that the KPI request is routed through a URL like: ".../dynamic_dest/<destination-rt>/<AppID>/~/odata/...".

Here, <destination-rt> is the destination that points to the HTML5 Application Repository runtime. However, the OData service used for the KPI is exposed by my CAP backend service (<destination-srv-api>), not by the HTML5 app runtime. If I call the backend OData endpoint directly (by removing the "/dynamic_dest/<destination-rt>" part of the URL), the service responds correctly. 

So my assumption is that the indicatorDataSource request is being resolved through the runtime destination of the HTML5 app instead of the backend destination that exposes the CAP service.

Has anyone encountered this behavior in a CDM-based Work Zone setup? Is there a recommended way to make the dynamic tile call the backend destination/service instead of the HTML5 runtime destination?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Clarification on cds.queued and same-db microservices when upgrading to CDS9
entry.content:
Hi,

We are upgrading a CAP Node.js project involving microservices sharing the same database container from CDS8 to CDS9 and we are having some issues with the Queueing with cds.queued feature. Specifically, since the transactional outbox which now is activated by default stores messages in the cds.outbox.Messages, we get the different microservices "polluting" each other with events belonging somewhere else (which often crashes the container completely due to hard errors such as required services not existing in the other container).

We are looking for clarification on:

  • We expect that setting "requires": { "queue": { "kind": "in-memory-queue" } }" (as shown in the documentation) brings it back to the default behavior in CDS8 (i.e. it is still delayed emission, but it happens without a persistent outbox meaning we may fail to emit the message).
  • Setting "requires": { "queue": { "kind": "in-memory-queue" } }" does not actually work. We needed to use "requires": { "kinds": { "queue": "in-memory-queue" } }" to make that work. That seems to be a bug in the default configuration (in-memory-queue refers back to the default queue configuration and inherits its kind -> i.e. it circles back to persistent-queue anyway). Is this known and being addressed, or are we maybe doing something wrong?
  • Is there a suggested pattern for using outboxes with same-db-microservices? The documentation for CAP Java indicates that this is a known limitation and suggests a manual approach, we do not see anything like that on the nodejs side though? Is that planned? Is there any proposed workaround (looking in the source code I do not see any environment variables or similar which would allow to e.g. use a dedicated outbox table per service)?
  • We have a lot of event driven data replication patterns which go like the bullets below without outboxes. If the event emission of Service B fails, the processing of the incoming event fails (despite the transaction actually having committed) and we can track that in the DMQ on the way into Service B. We rely on this to achieve "at least once" SLA. If we introduce an outbox we assume this no longer happens, instead it is the outbox which needs to be monitored and retried. Is this correct (i.e. if we start using such outboxes we must also start managing the dead-letter-queue to achieve the same SLA)?
    • Service A commits an update, then emits to eventmesh
    • Service B replicates the changed data from service A, does some processing on its own, commits that, then emits its own eventmesh message
    • Service C picks up the message and processes.

Any advice much appreciated, thanks in advance!

//Carl

entry.title: SAP Document Information Extraction (DIE)creates HTML5 App Repository binding expected behavior?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We have a CAP Node.js application deployed on SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry) that consumes multiple SAP Business Services, including:

  • SAP Document Information Extraction (DIE)
  • SAP Document Management Service (DMS / SDM)
  • SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)
  • XSUAA, Destination, HTML5 Application Repository, etc.

    When binding these services to our CAP application, we observe the following behavior:

    Observation

    In the application’s VCAP_SERVICES, the SAP Document Information Extraction (DIE) service binding contains the following attributes:

"credentials": {
  "sap.cloud.service": "com.sap.apps.documentinformationextraction",
  "saasregistryenabled": true,
  "html5-apps-repo": {
    "app_host_id": "<uuid>"
  }
}


Due to this, in the HTML5 Applications Repository cockpit, we see the warning:

Destination <…> points to the html5-apps-repo instance <…>, which does not contain any HTML5 applications.

At the same time:

  • Our own HTML5 application is deployed correctly to our  html5-apps-repo (app-host) service instance.
  • The application functions correctly, and the DIE UI works as expected

Additional Context
We also consume DMS and SBPA, and their service bindings also contain an HTML5 Application Repository, but no warning appears in those cases.

Based on SAP Help and Community blogs, our current understanding is:

  • SAP Business Services that expose UIs automatically provision and bind an HTML5 Application Repository via the service broker
  • The html5-apps-repo.app_host_id is injected into VCAP_SERVICES by the service itself, not by  MTA configuration
  • In the case of SAP Document Information Extraction, the UI is centrally managed / SaaS-based and not deployed as static HTML5 artifacts into the customer’s HTML5 repository, therefore the repository may appear empty

    Question to SAP Experts / SAP Team

Could you please confirm the following points:

  1.  Is it expected and by design that SAP Document Information Extraction creates and binds an html5-apps-repo entry in VCAP_SERVICES?
  2.  Is it correct that the DIE UI is not delivered as static HTML5 apps into the customer’s HTML5 Application Repository, which may cause the repository to appear empty?
  3. Is the warning shown in the HTML5 Application Repository cockpit in this case a known/expected side effect and not a deployment or configuration issue?
  4. Is there any SAP Note or official documentation that explicitly describes this behavior for DIE (or for UI‑exposing SAP Business Services in general)?


We want to be sure this behavior is supported and go‑live safe, and to have an SAP‑confirmable reference for audit/review purposes.

Many thanks in advance for your confirmation and guidance.

Best regards,
Raghav

entry.title: 502 Bad Gateway when embedding SAP UI5 App via Managed Approuter and IFrame
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am facing a 502 Bad Gateway error when attempting to embed an existing SAP UI5 application into a new UI5 project using an IFrame on SAP BTP.

The Scenario:

  1. App A (Source): An existing SAP UI5 application (URL provided to me).

  2. App B (Wrapper): A new SAP UI5 application deployed on BTP where I am trying to embed App A via an <iframe />.

  3. Destination: I have created a destination in the BTP Cockpit pointing to the hostname of App A with Authentication: NoAuthentication and Proxy Type: Internet.

Technical Implementation:

In my xs-app.json (Managed Approuter), I have defined the route as follows:

JSON
 
 
{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/sap/(.*)$",
      "target": "/sap/$1",
      "destination": "Test2",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

In my View Controller, I am setting the IFrame source dynamically: 

 
var sSrvUrl = sap.ui.require.toUrl("projectxxxxxxxxxx");
var surl = sSrvUrl + "/sap/xx/xxxxxx/xxxxxxxxxxx/index.html";

console.log("URL" + sFullUrl_1);

         

this.byId("html1").setContent(`
   <iframe 
                    src="${surl }"
                    width="100%"
                    height="500px"
                    style="border: none;">
                </iframe>
            `);

The Issue:

  • When I use a standard public URL (like https://en.wikipedia.org) in the destination and route, the IFrame loads correctly.

  • When I use the SAP UI5 application URL, the Approuter returns a 502 Bad Gateway.

Troubleshooting Done:

  • Confirmed the destination "Check Connection" returns 200 OK or 302.

  • Verified that the source UI5 app is accessible directly in the browser.

  • Checked for X-Frame-Options or Content-Security-Policy (CSP) headers; however, the error is a 502 (network/gateway level), not a 403 or a browser blocked error.

Question: Is there a specific configuration required in the destination or xs-app.json when the target is another SAP-hosted UI5 application? Could this be related to the Approuter failing to handle the redirect or a protocol mismatch (HTTP/HTTPS)?

Any insights would be greatly appreciated!

SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment 

@ArunJacob @junwu @georg_claude1

entry.title: HTML5 (Custom Fiori) app fails with 500 (token exchange) in TST only – works in DEV & ACC landscape
entry.content:
We have a custom HTML5 app deployed to the HTML5 Application Repository and launched via managed Launchpad / Work Zone.

  •  DEV – works
  • ACC – works
  • TST – fails

Same MTA, same UI artifacts, same configuration pattern across all landscapes.

Problem (TST only)

  • /index.html
  • /manifest.json
  • /Component-preload.js

All return:
HTTP 500 – Internal Server Error Failed to exchange login token by an XSUAA token Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'url') identityzone undefined / clientid undefined

This happens for all users and occurs before any backend (CAP) call.

What is already verified

  1. HTML5 app is correctly uploaded and visible via cf html5-list
  2. Single app ID
  3. public visibility
  4. HTML5 repository destination exists and matches ACC:
  • sap.cloud.service
  • uri / content_endpoint
  • app_host_id
  • xsappname
  • OAuth2ClientCredentials
  • HTML5 repo host service key contains valid UAA data
  • Work Zone content provider updated
  • Same issue when launching via HTML5 Applications (not Work Zone specific)
  • Other HTML5 apps run fine in the same TST subaccount

Key observation
Since static files fail, this looks like a managed approuter / Launchpad runtime token‑exchange failure, not a CAP or UI build issue.

Has anyone seen a case where:

  • HTML5 repo content is present and public
  • Repository destination & service key are correct
  • DEV/ACC work
  • but one landscape fails with 500 during managed runtime token exchange?

Is this a known:

  • Launchpad runtime issue?
  • HTML5 repository runtime issue?
  • Internal managed destination / UAA resolution issue?
  • Any pointers, SAP Notes, or similar experience would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance 

Regards,
Raghav

entry.title: Issue with Japanese IME Input Recognition in Value Help Search Field (Fiori Elements)
entry.content:
In a SAP Fiori Elements application running on SAPUI5 1.120.2, using OData V4 and CAPM (Nodejs) as backend service, we implement Value Help using annotations only (@Common.ValueList).


When users input Japanese text using IME in the search field inside the Value Help and perform conversion (space + enter), the latest converted value is not recognized by the search field for the first time. If user closes the Value Help dialog and enters the input again for the second time, the search field works as expected.


Unexpected result: 

  • The result list is not updated according to the latest input.

Expected Result: 

  • The result list should be updated immediately based on the final converted Japanese text, without requiring the dialog to be reopened.
Question:  Has anyone experienced this issue before? If so, how was it resolved? Any guidance would be appreciated.

entry.title: Table SAP MM
entry.content:
1. Données de base articles (Material Master):

  • MARA : Données générales articles (informations de base comme le type d'article, le groupe de marchandises, etc.)
  • MAKT : Désignations des articles (descriptions en différentes langues)
  • MARM : Unités de mesure pour l'article (unités de quantité, dimensions, etc.)
  • MBEW : Valorisation article (données comptables et de valorisation)
  • MARD : Données de stockage article par magasin (quantités en stock par emplacement)

2. Achats (Purchasing):

  • EKKO : En-tête de document d'achat (informations générales sur la commande d'achat)
  • EKPO : Poste de document d'achat (détails de chaque position de la commande)
  • EKET : Échéances du programme de livraison (dates de livraison prévues)
  • EINA : Fiche infos-achats - données générales (informations générales sur la relation fournisseur-article)
  • EINE : Fiche infos-ach. - données de l'organisation d'achat (données spécifiques à l'organisation d'achat)
  • EBAN : Demande d'achat (requête interne pour l'approvisionnement)

3. Mouvements de stock (Inventory Management):

  • MKPF : En-tête du document article (informations générales sur le mouvement de stock)
  • MSEG : Segment de document pour article (détails de chaque mouvement de stock)
  • MBLNR : Numéro de document article.
  • BWART : Type de mouvement.
  • LGORT : Emplacement de stockage.
  • MATNR : Numéro d'article.

4. Valorisation (Valuation):

  • MBEW : Valorisation article (données comptables et de valorisation, prix standard, prix moyen mobile, etc.)
  • CKMI : Historique des prix (historique des modifications de prix)

5. Planification des besoins (Material Requirements Planning - MRP):

  • MDKP : Données en-tête document de planification (informations générales sur l'exécution MRP)
  • MDTB : Table de planification (résultats du calcul des besoins)

6. Données générales et organisations:

  • T001W : Tables des usines
  • T024E : Organisations d'achat
  • T001L : Emplacements de stockage

Tables importantes pour les conditions de prix:

  • KONV : Conditions (données des conditions de prix)
  • KONP : Poste de condition (détails de chaque condition)
  • A001 à AXXX : Tables de conditions (dépendent du type de condition)
  • PRCD_ELEMENTS : Table de prix dans S/4HANA (simplifie la structure des données de prix)

Remarques importantes :

  • Cette liste n'est pas exhaustive, mais elle couvre les tables les plus couramment utilisées.
  • Dans S/4HANA, certaines tables ont été remplacées ou fusionnées pour simplifier le modèle de données. Par exemple, la table MATDOC remplace la combinaison de MKPF et MSEG pour les documents articles.
  • L'accès direct aux tables en production est généralement déconseillé. Il est préférable d'utiliser les transactions et les rapports standard de SAP ou de créer des requêtes personnalisées via des vues CDS (Core Data Services) dans S/4HANA.
  • La structure des tables et les noms des champs peuvent varier légèrement en fonction de la version de SAP utilisée.

entry.title: req object is not getting passed to bound action
entry.content:
Hi all,

I wanted to build several functions for my Service in CAP. Weirdly I can access the req object for bound actions of one entity without problems, but bound actions on another entity have only association/composition arguments.

ServiceDefinition cds

entity A as projection on my.A actions {
 action doSomethingA(); //works with req!
}

entity  B as projection on my.B actions{
 action doSomethingB();//does not work with req!
}

 in javascript I then extend the ApplicationService

class TestService extends cds.ApplicationService {
 init(){
  this.on('doSomethingA','A',this.doSomethingA);
  this.on('doSomethingB','B',this.doSomethingB);
 }

 async doSomethingA(req){
  //here i have access to the req object and can use something like req.params or req.subject
 }

 async doSomethingB(entityDefinition,a,c,d,b){
  //here i do NOT have access to the req object and CAN'T use something like req.params or req.subject
  // I need to use different variables for each entity in the Association/Composition chain. so for entities related like this A->C->D->B, I would use the variables above
 }
}

 

If you need any additional information, please let me know. I am really stuck/confused by it.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: I built a zero-dependency Excel plugin for SAP CAP — does this solve a problem you have too?
entry.content:
Every time I needed to generate .xlsx files in a CAP project the answer was the same: pull in exceljs or xlsx, wire up the API, and deal with the bundle weight. I decided to solve it differently.

I built @cap-js/excel — a CAP plugin that generates Excel workbooks using only Node.js built-ins (zlib and buffer). No external runtime dependencies at all.

It's not public yet. Before publishing I want to know if this is just a problem I have or if it resonates with more people.


HOW IT WORKS

Install the package and it auto-discovers itself as a CAP plugin — no manual require in your handlers. The full API is available under cds.excel.


Entity-driven tables — the part I use most

The thing that saves the most time: pass a CDS entity and the plugin builds the columns automatically. It reads your entity's elements, skips associations and compositions, and generates one column per primitive field. It picks up @title and @Common.Label annotations as column headers and maps CDS types to the right Excel cell styles out of the box.

    srv.on('exportReport', async (req) => {
      const { ExcelBuilder } = cds.excel
      const rows = await SELECT.from('MyService.Orders').orderBy('createdAt desc')

      const wb = new ExcelBuilder()
      wb.addSheet('Orders').addEntity(srv.entities.Orders, {
        rows,
        labels:     true,    // column headers from @title / @Common.Label
        autoFormat: true,    // integer, decimal, date styles from CDS types
        exclude:    ['internalField', 'technicalKey'],
        order:      ['orderDate', 'customer', 'total'],
        overrides:  { total: { header: 'Total (USD)' } },
        theme:      'blue',
      })

      return wb
    })

CDS type to Excel style mapping:

Integer, Int16, Int32, Int64, UInt8 → Whole number
Decimal, Double → 2 decimal places
Date, DateTime, Timestamp → Short date
Everything else → General

If you prefer incremental configuration there is a fluent EntityBuilder that defers rendering until serialisation time:

const cfg = sheet.getEntity(srv.entities.Orders)
cfg
.setLabels(true)
.setAutoFormat(true)
.include(['orderDate', 'customer', 'amount', 'status'])
.setOrder(['orderDate', 'customer'])
.addRows(rows)

Pair it with the @Excel.export annotation and the download becomes fully declarative — annotate the action in your .cds model, return the ExcelBuilder instance from the handler, and the plugin intercepts the result and sends the binary response automatically:

    // in your .cds model
    @Excel.export: { filename: 'orders.xlsx' }
    action exportOrders();

    // in your handler
    srv.on('exportOrders', async (req) => {
      const wb = new ExcelBuilder()
      wb.addSheet('Orders').addEntity(srv.entities.Orders, {
        rows:       await SELECT.from('MyService.Orders'),
        labels:     true,
        autoFormat: true,
      })
      return wb  // plugin handles the binary response
    })
 

Tests:

This is not mocked or manually created — it is a direct output from the current implementation.


Other features

- Multi-sheet workbooks — add as many named sheets as you need per workbook
- 8 colour themes (blue, green, orange, red, purple, dark, light, default) with header rows and alternating stripes
- Formula support — SUM, PRODUCT, cell multiply, and arbitrary inline formulas
- Three download patterns — Base64 string for OData actions, direct binary via cds.excel.download(), or fully declarative via @Excel.export
- Worksheet protection — sheet.protect({ password }) to prevent accidental edits
- Sensitivity labels — embed classification metadata as custom document properties
- Structured logging — integrates with cds.log('excel'), falls back to console.* outside CAP
- Deterministic output — fixed timestamps produce byte-identical ZIP archives across builds
- Standalone — works without CAP via require('@cap-js/excel')


WHY NO DEPENDENCIES?

exceljs is around 200 KB minified and pulls sub-dependencies. xlsx has a dual license that can get complicated in corporate environments. Since .xlsx is just a ZIP of XML files with an open spec (OOXML / ECMA-376), implementing the essentials with Node's native zlib is doable — and it removes any supply chain, license, or bundle-size conversation entirely.


IS THIS USEFUL TO YOU?

A few questions before I decide whether to publish:

- Do you use CAP and need to generate Excel today? How are you solving it?
- Does the zero-dependency angle matter in your context — audits, license policies, bundle size?
- Is there a feature that would make this actually useful for you that isn't listed here?

Happy to share more details or a demo. Any feedback appreciated.
